keyFactors.approximateWindowPerTimeStep: Often described as approximately two weeks in public explanations; use as demo temporal window, not exact timestamp evidence.
keyFactors.featureDisplayPolicy: Raw anonymous features are not displayed directly; they are transformed into review signals and evidence fields.

labelExplanation.licit_for_evaluation_only: Original Elliptic label used only to evaluate or explain the demo sample. A licit-labeled transaction can still be selected for high-priority review if rule-based graph signals are elevated; that is a review queue signal, not a final illicit decision.
labelExplanation.illicit_for_evaluation_only: Original Elliptic label used only for research/demo evaluation. The notebook does not use it as a final AML conclusion.
labelExplanation.unknown: Unlabeled transaction in the Elliptic dataset; useful for review-priority style demo cases.

graphSummary.note: Weak connected components are intentionally not computed in the notebook lite path to avoid collecting the full edge list into Python memory.

generatedDemoCaseSummary.samplingPolicy: Multi-batch stratified merge; deduplicated by rawTxId.

ruleAndReviewSignals: retrievedGuidance from FATF and FinCEN PDFs

limitations: Transaction-level graph review only; not wallet/address-level audit.
No KYC, source-of-funds, customer identity, sanctions-list matching, or wallet attribution is available in the public dataset.
BTC amount fields shown in front-end schema are null because the public Elliptic feature CSV uses anonymized features.
Distance to illicit cluster is local graph exposure within the prepared sample, not full chain tracing.
Rule-based score is a prioritization signal, not a probability of crime and not a final compliance decision.
Weak connected components are not computed in lite mode to keep the notebook runnable in Kaggle/Colab memory limits.
